reviews.title: Spotify is the best
reviews.text: Spotify is easily the most essential app on my iPhone. If you want the best way to stream music, discover new artists, and share tracks with friends, this is it.Why it’s worth the download:Unmatched Algorithm: The "Discover Weekly" and "Daily Mix" features are incredibly accurate at finding new music tailored to your exact taste.Flawless iOS Integration: The app is fast, clean, and supports seamless Handoff from your iPhone to your Mac, iPad, or HomePod.Social Sharing: It is super easy to make collaborative playlists with friends and share tracks directly to your Instagram or Snapchat stories.The AI DJ: This feature mixes your top tracks with new recommendations, complete with realistic, radio-style commentary.The only catch:The free tier is pretty limited with unskippable ads. If you want the best experience, upgrading to Premium is a must—but between the massive catalog and offline downloading, it is worth every penny.

reviews.title: App is falling off…
reviews.text: Been on Spotify for over 10 years. The app performance is a joke now. Takes 5+ mins just to get the app up and running most of the time and there are several glitches that require you to close the app and then start and wait again. Also - make it an option in settings to make podcasts auto play on audio only. Loading video often slows things down, but this is not just a podcast issue. If it weren’t for the algorithm which is incredible for music discovery, I would have already left. Now with websites that help create playlists for Apple Music from your Spotify ones, though, it is in consideration if this app does not improve.

reviews.title: Pretty good
reviews.text:
Spotify is such a good app and it is so easy to listen to so much of your favorite music just by putting in an email. The good thing about Spotify is that no matter how much playlists or music you have in your library, the app doesn’t glitch or mess up. You can have as much playlists as you want and it will still work just as well. One thing from Spotify that is a little annoying is how much ads there are though;I get that ads are critical for revenue and also beneficial for both the seller and Spotify, but if you don’t have premium, the ads can be really annoying, especially looking at how much there is. The more I listen to my music, the more
And more ads I get more often, and honestly, I think that Spotify could be a little softer on the ads. Now I am not the type of person to say that Spotify should have barely any ads because I understand their importance. I’m just saying that since I’m getting 3 ads after 2 songs and then I get to listen to 2 more songs and then I have 2 ads, it just seems a little bit too much. I just think that every 4 songs there should be 3 ads. Idk I guess I’m a little picky I just don’t know why I listen to 2 or three songs and then have 2,3, or 4 ads that are even longer than the songs themselves. One thing about the ads that I do like though is that they are really good at producing small business ads rather than well known franchise ads and some ads even have polls for us to interact with them. No hard feelings about it though because Spotify has given me the best and biggest music experience without pay and I will always love it🫰🏻🫰🏻

reviews.title: Free tier on iPhone is deliberately worse and Spotify admits it
reviews.text:
Spotify’s free experience is NOT equal across devices, and that’s unacceptable. On Windows, macOS, and even iPadOS, free users get full on-demand listening. On iPhone and Android? You get a daily on-demand cap — once you hit it, Spotify forces you into shuffle mode, even mid-playlist. Spotify’s own community moderators openly confirmed: “The iPad has some advantages over the mobile version, just like the desktop apps.”

Mobile free users are also stuck at 160kbps audio quality while desktop free users get better. This isn’t a technical limitation — it’s a deliberate design choice to pressure mobile users into paying. If you’re happy to listen free on your Mac or iPad, great. The moment you open the iPhone app, you’re a second-class user.

One star until the free tier is consistent across all platforms. The double standard is insulting.

I have tried other apps and while they may have some cool features Spotify is still the best in my opinion. I have made suggestions before and surprisingly I was listened to and things were added. I am hoping that if I share another maybe Spotify will find a way to implement it as well. Does anyone remember those computer screen savers that used to either move to the music you had on or would change color to the beat? It looked something from a geometry class equation. I think that would be a really cool feature to add so when you are listening and just wanna zone out you could have that on your screen. Thanks for a great app Spotify!!!!
